## Qaio 0.4.10

Fixes workspace creation on Windows, makes the sidebar readable, and cleans up the terminology across the board.

### What's new

- **Workspace creation works on Windows.** The engine used `$HOME` for the home directory, which does not exist on Windows. Now uses the `dirs` crate for cross-platform resolution (`%USERPROFILE%` on Windows, `$HOME` on Unix).
- **Gemini provider fully wired.** The setup wizard and brain-connect step now detect and display Gemini alongside Anthropic and OpenAI. Previously, connecting Gemini would not reflect in the UI until a restart.
- **Sidebar workspace title is visible.** The workspace name in the sidebar was rendered in `text-foreground` (same color as the sidebar background in light mode). Now uses `text-sidebar-foreground` so it's always readable.
- **"Mission Control" renamed to "Command Center".** All references to "missions" in the UI now say "tasks" (English), "tareas" (Spanish), or "tarefas" (Portuguese). The top-level nav reads "Command Center" / "Centro de comando" in all three locales.
- **Removed coming-soon providers.** The provider picker no longer shows placeholder cards for providers that are not yet available. Only Anthropic, OpenAI, and Gemini appear.
- **New branding assets.** Qaio SQ mark replaces the legacy logo SVGs throughout the app.
- **Knowledge base tab for agents.** Agents now have a dedicated knowledge base surface for capturing learnings from projects, meetings, and daily work.

### Reliability

- **No more fire-and-forget on workspace creation.** The "Create workspace" button now shows a spinner, disables during creation, and surfaces errors as toasts instead of silently failing.

### Before you upgrade

- **Mac:** quit Qaio before installing. macOS does not always replace a running app cleanly.
- **Windows:** the MSI is still not OS-code-signed. One SmartScreen "Run anyway" prompt on first install only.

### If you are on 0.4.5 or later

Qaio should detect 0.4.10, download it, install it, and relaunch into the new version on its own.

### Known limitations

- **Windows ARM64 not yet shipped.** v0.4.10 is x64-only on Windows.
- **Windows MSI not yet OS-code-signed.** SignPath integration still pending.
- **"Continue with Microsoft" sign-in remains hidden** while we reconcile the Supabase Azure provider with the production callback URL. Google sign-in is unaffected.
